assertions.reason: Browser validation could not reliably drive the Phaser canvas to a fresh new game in this isolated session; code-backed verification confirms all five zone mini-bosses are elite, have 3+ Chinese pre-fight dialogue lines, persist non-repeat tracking via miniBossDialogueSeen, and define guaranteed enhanced loot tables.

Real browser collection flow was blocked by fragile canvas interaction, but code-backed verification confirms each zone has at least 4 lore collectibles with distinct visuals, Chinese lore text well over 50 characters, hidden/non-obvious placement flags, lore popups, and lore log progress strings like "X/Y 收集" in the UI.

Could not run enough exploratory movement through the Phaser canvas to trigger live events, so verification remained code-backed: RandomEventSystem enforces a 30000ms cooldown, blocks in safe zones and combat, defines ambush and treasure cache events with Chinese warnings, and ZoneScene routes those events into gameplay/log output.

Live merchant/rescue/puzzle event execution was blocked by the same Phaser canvas control limitation, but code-backed verification shows wandering merchant, rescue, and environmental puzzle events are implemented with zone-scaled data, Chinese UI/log strings, shop payload wiring, rescue rewards, and puzzle interaction handling.

The browser session reached the game shell but reliable in-canvas travel/interaction to NPCs, hidden areas, and sub-dungeon portals was not achievable. Code-backed verification confirms every zone has 2 new NPCs with Chinese dialogue, 1 hidden area with reward/discovery text, 3 story decorations, and at least 2 zones expose sub-dungeon entrances with enter/exit flow and companion-carrying scene restart data.

Code-backed audit found the new story-content data and UI strings in Chinese (Simplified) across mini-boss dialogue, lore text, hidden areas, random events, NPC names/dialogue, sub-dungeon names, and story decoration metadata, with dedicated tests asserting Chinese content.

frictions: agent-browser opened the app at http://localhost:5173/abyssfire/ and screenshots worked, but Phaser canvas clicks were not reliable enough to guarantee a fresh new-game interaction path or deterministic in-world traversal.
The runtime game instance was not exposed on window/Phaser.GAMES in this build, so user-surface checks had to rely on screenshots plus code-backed verification instead of runtime introspection.
blockers: Assigned browser validation for Phaser canvas flows was partially blocked by opaque canvas interactions; assertions needing actual traversal/combat/event triggering remain blocked rather than guessed.
summary: Validated six assigned story-world assertions. VAL-STORY-012 passed via code-backed audit; VAL-STORY-002/003/004/005/010 are blocked for real-user-surface confirmation because the Phaser canvas could not be driven reliably enough in this session, though source inspection confirms the underlying implementations and wiring exist.
